cards.back: Av = Av1 × Av2 (loaded gains). Gains multiply for cascaded stages.
Stage 2's Rin appears in || with stage 1's RC. Effective load = RC1 || Rin2. Reduces stage 1 gain.

cards.front: Why use CC as buffer stage?
cards.back: CC: Av≈1, very high Rin (minimal loading), very low Rout (drives loads). Ideal impedance matcher.
= Rin of first stage. Source only sees stage 1's input.
= Rout of last stage. Load only sees the final stage's output.
Gain↓, stability↑, bandwidth↑, distortion↓. Trades gain for quality.
1. Where is Rf connected at output? (collector=voltage, emitter=current). 2. How does Rf connect at input? (series=adds voltage, shunt=adds current).
Rf from Q2 collector (voltage sampling) to Q1 base (shunt mixing) → Voltage-Shunt (Shunt-Shunt). Rin↓, Rout↓.
Loading: Rf as passive resistor in || at input/output. Signal: modification of gain by feedback loop. Separate effects.
